original_background_story: Angio is an amateur singer who resembles a young Jezz Torrent (as he appeared in GTA Vice City) and even sings one of his solos. The Judges initially insult William after his performance with two of the judges voting it "Shame" but after Lazlow begs everyone to do something controversial to get ratings, Hugh Harrison votes William "fame" and the other judges follow suit despite already making their votes. Hugh then asks William to punch Lazlow, which he does twice.
generated_background_story: Nico Alvarez grew up on the lower decks of the sun‑soaked metropolis, dreaming of fame on the cheap reality TV circuit that dominated the city’s airwaves. He achieved brief stardom as a flamboyant host of “Skyline Scramble,” but the show's collapse amid economic turmoil left him disillusioned and indebted to shadowy sponsors. Determined to expose the corrupt media moguls, Nico repurposed his on‑camera tech into a toolkit for hacking and covert information gathering. He now leads a loose collective of “Signal Runners,” smuggling data between the affluent towers and the struggling districts. Beneath his charismatic exterior, Nico battles guilt over the lives he once exploited for entertainment. The city’s precarious economy fuels his urgency, as each broadcast threatens to drown the populace in further desperation. His choices will either ignite a grassroots uprising or cement the media’s tyrannical grip on the metropolis.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Flash Capture: Instantly records a short holographic clip of nearby activity, revealing hidden clues.
  - Street Sprint: Increases movement speed by 15% when moving through crowded streets.
  - Data Scramble: Briefly disrupts enemy surveillance drones within a small radius.
- Tier 2:
  - Holo Decoy: Projects a temporary illusion of Nico to distract opponents.
  - Neon Blade: Unleashes a quick melee strike with the hover‑blade, dealing moderate damage and briefly blinding the target.
  - Broadcast Hijack: Overrides a nearby advertisement screen to display a message, lowering enemy morale.
- Tier 3:
  - Skyhook Leap: Launches Nico upward from any surface, covering large distances and evading attacks.
  - Network Overload: Sends a powerful pulse through city infrastructure, temporarily disabling all electronic devices in a wide area.
  - Reputation Ripple: Gains the trust of a faction, unlocking unique dialogue options and resources throughout the city.

original_background_story:
Johnny Joestar is the protagonist of Steel Ball Run and a minor character in JoJolion. He is the seventh JoJo of the JoJo's Bizarre Adventure series. 
 
Johnny is a young man of below-average height and slim to medium build. He has light eyes, and light, shoulder-length hair curling upwards at its ends. 
 
Johnny always wears a knit capW, printed with many small five-pointed stars. His hair sticks out of holes on either side of the top, taking a shape like short horns. A horseshoe hangs from the front; framing an image of the dark silhouette of the profile of a horse's reared head. 
 
Johnny wears similarly star-patterned pants; of a piece with footwear bearing spursW. He commonly wears a light-colored, hooded, short-sleeved top; printed with thick lines in the approximate formation of a harness (later, with patches in the shape of hearts adorning both shoulders). A long feather (bordered by black with one black spot at its tip) emerges from under the back of his collar; and he wears wrist cuffs patterned with stars and stripes. 
 
Johnny Joestar is a depressive ex-jockey, who clings to his hope of regaining the use of his legs through the Spin and then the Corpse Part throughout Steel Ball Run. 
 
Johnny is introduced as a distant paraplegic man whose career was ruined when he was shot in the spine after conceitedly antagonizing a passerby. After Johnny witnesses Gyro's Steel Balls and Spin, and how they enabled him to stand up on his feet for a moment, he becomes determined to follow him and make him teach the Spin, then after discovering the Corpse Part making his legs twitch, to acquire them.
generated_background_story: Kairo Tenrai hails from the floating citadel of Luminara, a city of scholars who fused ancient mysticism with cutting‑edge nanotech. Trained as the heir to the Guardian Blade, he swore to protect the balance between the arcane and the mechanical. When a rogue faction shattered the Nexus Crystal, dark energy began corrupting the world’s storylines, pulling heroes and villains from every era into chaotic battles. Kairo now travels across the shattered arcs, seeking the scattered crystal fragments to restore order. He is haunted by visions of a future where his own power becomes the very source of the corruption he fights. Driven by duty and a personal promise to his late mentor, he must master both his blade and the latent energy within his scar before the manga’s pages tear completely apart.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Blade Spark: A quick slash that releases a burst of cyan energy, dealing low damage and briefly stunning the opponent.
  - Circuit Dash: A short forward dash that leaves a trail of electric particles, evading attacks and closing distance.
  - Neon Guard: Passive aura that slightly reduces damage taken from special moves.
- Tier 2:
  - Celestial Arc: A mid‑range projectile that arcs upward before descending, hitting multiple times.
  - Resonant Counter: When timed with an incoming hit, counterattacks with a burst of energy, dealing moderate damage.
  - Photon Pulse: Emits a pulse that slows enemy movement speed for a short duration.
- Tier 3:
  - Skyward Ascension: A powerful upward leaping attack that launches Kairo into the air and slams down with a shockwave.
  - Eternity Slash: Charged heavy strike that penetrates defenses, dealing high damage and briefly disabling the opponent’s special abilities.
  - Sovereign Resolve: Ultimate buff that for a few seconds doubles attack damage and grants immunity to stun while a luminous aura surrounds Kairo.

original_background_story: Arthur Morgan is a central character and the main protagonist of Red Dead Redemption 2. Arthur Morgan was born circa 1863 to Beatrice and Lyle Morgan, somewhere in the northern United States. Around 1877, Arthur was found and picked up of the streets by Dutch van der Linde and Hosea Matthews.
generated_background_story: Born in the soot‑choked slums of the river district, Rook Vale learned early that the law favored the wealthy factories over the starving masses. After his sister vanished under the orders of the notorious Iron Syndicate, he swore vengeance and escaped the city’s clutches, becoming a wanted outlaw. Rook now roams the frontier rail lines and forgotten outposts, stealing supplies to fund his search while staying one step ahead of the Syndicate’s relentless enforcers. He is haunted by guilt for the collateral damage his raids cause, fearing he becomes the very monster he despises. The dawn of the modern age brings steam‑driven locomotives and early electricity, shifting the balance of power and offering new tools for both his rebellion and the Syndicate’s reach. As the Syndicate tightens its grip, Rook must decide whether to lead a full‑scale uprising or vanish into legend, leaving the oppressed to fend for themselves.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:  
  - Quickdraw: Reduces weapon draw time by 20 % and adds a brief damage boost on the first shot.  
  - Streetwise: Increases loot found in urban environments by 15 % and reveals hidden pathways.  
  - Evasion Roll: Grants a short dash that makes Rook untargetable for 0.5 seconds.  
- Tier 2:  
  - Steam‑Powered Grapple: Fires a short‑range hook that pulls Rook toward metallic surfaces, enabling traversal and surprise attacks.  
  - Reinforced Coat: Reduces incoming damage by 10 % and adds a small health regeneration when staying still.  
  - Dual‑Shot Burst: Fires both pistols simultaneously for a rapid, high‑damage volley with a minor spread.  
- Tier 3:  
  - Syndicate Disruptor: Deploys a timed device that disables nearby Syndicate automatons for 5 seconds.  
  - Ghost Walk: Grants temporary invisibility while crouched, lasting 3 seconds or until Rook attacks.  
  - Revolution’s Call: Boosts allied NPC morale in the vicinity, increasing their attack speed and damage by 25 % for 8 seconds.

original_background_story: The mascot species of the Pokémon franchise, Pikachu is an Electric-type rodent that evolves from Pichu and can later evolve into Raichu. Pikachu has been extensively featured in nearly every piece of Pokémon media, and its popularity extends outside of its home series - it has been playable in every Super Smash Bros. game, painted on commercial airliners, and has its own balloon in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade.
generated_background_story: Kael Varyn was born in the shadow of the Grand Colosseum, where his parents served as keepers of the ancient bestiary. From a young age he was taught to respect both the fury and the grace of the creatures that fought within the arena’s walls. When a mysterious artifact known only as the “Heart of the Primordials” was stolen, the tournaments were plunged into chaos, and Kael swore to retrieve it to restore balance. His internal conflict stems from the lingering doubt that he relies too heavily on his creatures, fearing he may become a puppet rather than a master. Determined to prove his worth, Kael enters all four tournaments, seeking the secret mode that promises the toughest trials. Each victory brings him closer to confronting the ultimate creature warrior that guards the artifact, and to uncovering the truth behind the secret battles. Failure would not only condemn the beasts he loves but also allow the artifact’s power to fall into the hands of a hidden cabal.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Ember Lash: Summon a small flame spirit that lashes a single enemy for modest damage.
  - Reflexive Parry: Gain a brief window to automatically block the next incoming attack.
  - Beast Whisper: Slightly increase the accuracy and critical chance of summoned creatures.
- Tier 2:
  - Adaptive Tactics: Reduce cooldowns on all abilities when a creature is defeated.
  - Elemental Bond: Allow two summoned creatures to share elemental affinity, boosting their defenses.
  - Redirect Attack: Transfer a portion of damage taken by Kael to his active summoned creature.
- Tier 3:
  - Ultimate Fusion: Merge Kael with his strongest creature for a single powerful melee strike.
  - Legendary Command: Grant all summoned creatures a temporary boost in speed and damage.
  - Rift of the Ancients: Open a short‑lived portal that summons a random elite creature to aid in battle.

original_background_story: His past is not known but he is known to the public as an important government official and the Director of FEMA. He was appointed to FEMA through Page's manipulation of an unnamed senator infected with the Gray Death, and went to work immediately. A cloak-and-dagger man, Simons was the first higher-up on the scene after JC Denton's capture of NSF operatives in Battery Park; his interrogation ended with Simons personally executing both prisoners.
generated_background_story: Kade Arlen was born in the industrial slums of New Osaka, where the line between flesh and machine was blurred by necessity. After a fatal accident that claimed his family, he volunteered for the Global Counter‑Terror Directorate (GCTD), receiving nanite‑enhanced augmentations that saved his life but tethered his mind to a constant digital hum. Assigned to the anti‑terrorist unit “Aegis Vector,” he is tasked with repelling a coordinated assault on the orbital megastructure Aurora Hub by the radical group “The Crimson Veil.” As Kade infiltrates the besieged site, fragmented data streams reveal that both the Veil and a hidden faction within GCTD are manipulating the conflict for a far‑reaching agenda. Haunted by the memories of his loss and the growing evidence that his own augmentations may be a conduit for control, he grapples with questions of free will, loyalty, and the morality of sacrificing lives for a perceived greater good. The fate of Aurora Hub—and perhaps the fragile peace of the entire world—rests on whether Kade can reconcile his engineered obedience with his emerging conscience. His decisions will shape the balance between surveillance‑driven order and the chaotic yearning for true autonomy.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Neural Quickdraw: Reduces weapon draw and aim time by 20%.
  - Nano Shield: Generates a temporary barrier that absorbs 15% of incoming damage for 6 seconds.
  - Moral Lens: Unlocks dialogue options that question orders, influencing faction reputation.
- Tier 2:
  - Echo Pulse: Emits a nanite pulse that reveals hidden enemies and traps within a 10‑meter radius.
  - Adaptive Camouflage: Grants short‑term invisibility while standing still, lasting up to 4 seconds.
  - Philosopher's Resolve: Reduces sanity loss from horror events by 30%.
- Tier 3:
  - Quantum Overdrive: Doubles movement speed and weapon damage for 5 seconds, with a cooldown of 45 seconds.
  - Conscience Override: Hacks enemy AI to temporarily turn a target hostile to its allies.
  - Worldweight Insight: Unlocks a unique narrative branch that can sway multiple factions simultaneously.

original_background_story: Peach (or Princess Peach) is a main protagonist in the Mario series. She's usually the damsel-in-distress of the games, who the players have to rescue, but she also has some important roles as the hero herself. She's the adorable and cute princess of the Mushroom Kingdom.
generated_background_story: Arin Varela grew up in the coastal town of Windspire, where he spent childhood evenings listening to his inventor mother’s tales of distant skies and forgotten relics. When a mysterious map surfaced, leading to the legendary Sky‑forge Airship, Arin seized the chance to prove himself as more than a dockyard apprentice. He discovered an ancient device that lets him temporarily bind the essence of objects, beasts, and foes, turning them into loyal allies for a short time. Driven by a promise to his sister—who was promised in marriage to the Airship’s authoritarian captain—Arin must gather the scattered Aether Crystals to restore the ship’s heart and free her from a forced union. Along the way, he befriends a shy wind‑sprite and a reprogrammed combat drone, learning that true power comes from the bonds he forges. Yet the lingering doubt that he may be chasing a myth haunts him, pushing him to prove the Sky‑forge’s existence once and for all.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - **Essence Latch**: Capture a nearby object or small creature for 5 seconds, gaining a minor combat boost.
  - **Echo Dash**: Short burst of forward momentum that leaves a translucent after‑image, evading attacks.
  - **Gadget Deploy**: Place a temporary turret that fires low‑damage projectiles at nearby enemies.
- Tier 2:
  - **Spectral Bind**: Extend capture duration to 10 seconds and grant the bound target a defensive shield.
  - **Wind‑Powered Leap**: Higher jump powered by backpack thrusters, allowing access to elevated platforms.
  - **Resource Siphon**: Harvest ambient energy from captured foes to restore a small portion of health.
- Tier 3:
  - **Aether Convergence**: Merge multiple captured essences into a powerful blast that damages all enemies in range.
  - **Airship Override**: Temporarily commandeer the Sky‑forge’s systems, granting a massive speed boost and invulnerability for 3 seconds.
  - **Legacy Recall**: Release all bound allies simultaneously, each delivering a unique effect based on its original type.

original_background_story: Mileena is a character in the Mortal Kombat series. A genetic experiment created by the sorcerer Shang Tsung, she is one of the adopted daughters of the Outworld Emperor Shao Kahn and one of his personal enforcers. She has served as an antagonist throughout the series.
generated_background_story: Kairo Vex was once a promising engineer in the floating metropolis of Zircon Spire, specializing in kinetic weaponry. After a catastrophic experiment fused his nervous system with prototype energy conduits, he gained the ability to channel electricity through his body but lost control over the volatile power. Driven to master his newfound abilities, Kairo entered the tournament to locate the ancient Core Matrix rumored to stabilize his condition. Yet the Core also promises untold power, forcing him to choose between personal salvation and the risk of unleashing a city‑wide catastrophe. Haunted by visions of his former self, he fights not only opponents but the inner surge that threatens to consume him. His journey intertwines with the tournament’s chaotic stages, where every bout could bring him closer to redemption or ruin.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Pulse Jab: A quick light punch that briefly stuns the opponent.
  - Shock Step: A low dash that leaves an electric trail damaging anyone who steps on it.
  - Blade Snap: A fast melee strike with the energy blade that reduces opponent’s block meter.
- Tier 2:
  - Arc Uppercut: A powerful upward punch that launches the foe and builds X‑Ray meter faster.
  - Tesla Teleport: Short-range teleport that instantly repositions and deals minor shock damage on arrival.
  - Overload EX: Enhanced EX move that adds a burst of area‑wide electricity to any special attack.
- Tier 3:
  - Core Burst: An X‑Ray move that channels the Core Matrix, delivering a massive multi‑hit combo with visual distortion.
  - Circuit Breaker: A combo breaker that nullifies opponent’s ongoing combo and resets their meter.
  - Voltaic Finale: A fatality where Kairo summons a storm of electric arcs, disintegrating the opponent in a dazzling display.

original_background_story:
Commander Shepard is the player character in BioWare's Mass Effect game trilogy: Mass Effect, Mass Effect 2, and Mass Effect 3. A veteran soldier of the Systems Alliance Navy military and N7-graduate of the Interplanetary Combatives Training (ICT) program, and the first human Citadel Council Spectre, Shepard works to stop "the Reapers", a sentient synthetic-machine race dedicated to wiping out all organic life.

Shepard's gender, class, first name and general appearance are chosen and customized by the player. Since the player can choose the gender of Shepard, much of the dialogue revolving around the character is gender neutral with only a few exceptions.
generated_background_story: Rhea Voss grew up on the remote farming colony of Verdant Reach, learning to tend crops under harsh radiation storms and to pilot the old supply rigs that kept the settlement alive. When the outpost was threatened by a marauding fleet of the Dominion Cartel, she volunteered for the newly formed Expeditionary Guard and quickly rose to become the Executive Officer of the flagship Solaris Rift. Haunted by the memory of her sister lost in the first raid, Rhea balances a fierce drive to protect civilians with a growing suspicion of the political machinations behind the war. Her tactical brilliance earned her the nickname “The Thorn,” as she can both nurture her squad and prune enemy formations with equal precision. Now she must unite disparate alien allies, uncover ancient relics hidden in radioactive wastelands, and decide whether to obey the chain of command or follow a covert beacon left by her late sister. The fate of the galaxy hinges on the choices she makes while steering an elite squad through relentless biotic assaults and heavily armored Citadel forces.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Pulse Disruptor: Fires a short‑range burst that temporarily disables enemy shields.
  - Tactical Scan: Reveals enemy positions and weak points in a 10‑meter radius for 5 seconds.
  - Field Medkit: Instantly restores a small amount of health to a nearby ally.
- Tier 2:
  - Biotic Suppression Field: Generates a stationary field that reduces biotic damage taken by allies within it by 25%.
  - Adaptive Reload: Decreases weapon reload time by 15% after each successful kill.
  - Hardened Resolve: Grants a temporary boost to Rhea’s armor when health drops below 30%.
- Tier 3:
  - Nova Strike: Unleashes a charged radial explosion dealing massive damage to all enemies in close proximity.
  - Command Aura: Increases all squad members’ attack speed and movement speed by 20% while Rhea is in leadership mode.
  - Echo of the Fallen: Calls forth a holographic echo of her sister that distracts enemies and provides a shield to allies for a short duration.

original_background_story: Link is the main protagonist in The Legend of Zelda series. He's usually the hero of the game, and the player's avatar.
generated_background_story: Lyra Pulse grew up in the humming corridors of the Resonance Forge, a settlement built atop the world’s forgotten drumbeats. As a child, she discovered an ancient relic—a treble‑clef staff—that syncs her movements with the planet’s hidden rhythm. When a discordant shadow spreads from the Core Echo, corrupting the world’s natural beats, Lyra vows to restore harmony. She joins the quest to traverse randomly generated soundscapes and dungeons, using her rhythm‑fueled abilities to outpace the encroaching silence. Haunted by the memory of her mentor’s last, unfinished melody, she feels the pressure to finish what was started. Each successful beat brings her closer to healing the Core Echo and saving the realm’s pulse. Yet the deeper she ventures, the more she learns that the true enemy may be the silence within herself.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Pulse Strike: Deals damage proportional to the player’s current beat meter.
  - Echo Guard: Grants a temporary shield that absorbs one hit, synchronized to the next downbeat.
  - Beat Boost: Increases movement speed for a short duration after landing on a musical tile.
- Tier 2:
  - Rhythm Dash: Performs a rapid dash that cuts through enemies, resetting the beat timer.
  - Harmonic Wave: Emits a radial wave that stuns nearby foes on the strong beat.
  - Sync Step: Allows a second jump in mid‑air if executed precisely on a sync point.
- Tier 3:
  - Crescendo Blast: Unleashes a powerful blast that scales with accumulated rhythm energy, clearing the screen of minor enemies.
  – Reverberation Shield: Creates a lasting aura that reflects projectiles when the player maintains perfect timing.
  – Finale Strike: A finishing move that deals massive damage when the final note of a track is reached.

original_background_story: Prince of Ylisse
generated_background_story: Varyn Kestrel was born in the ember‑shrouded citadel of Ashenhold, a frontier outpost that fell to a horde of necrotic marauders when he was a child. Rescued by a wandering order of knight‑mages, he was trained in both blade and arcane discipline, vowing to prevent any other village from sharing his loss. As the looming war between rival kingdoms and the rising dead intensifies, Varyn now leads a coalition of knights, mages, and archers, using his tactical mind and charismatic leadership to unite fractured forces. He carries the weight of his fallen comrades, haunted by the oath he swore to protect the living, yet struggles with the temptation to wield forbidden necromantic rites that could turn the tide. The fate of the burning world hinges on his ability to balance honor with desperate necessity, forging alliances while confronting the darkness within himself.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Blade Initiate: Basic melee attack deals moderate damage and briefly stuns enemies.
  - Rally Cry: Emits a short-range shout that grants nearby allies a small speed boost.
  - Ember Guard: Raises a temporary shield that absorbs a fixed amount of damage.
- Tier 2:
  - Twin Strike: Performs a rapid double‑slash, increasing damage against armored foes.
  - Bonded Pair: Links with a nearby ally, sharing a portion of incoming damage.
  - Arcane Ward: Infuses the shield with magic, reflecting a percentage of magical attacks.
- Tier 3:
  - Warhorn Resonance: Activates a global aura that enhances all allies' attack power for a short duration.
  - Deathbound Synergy: Combines with a necromancer ally to momentarily turn defeated enemies into temporary allies.
  - Unyielding Stand: Grants immunity to crowd‑control effects and massive damage reduction for a few seconds.

original_background_story: Ragna the Bloodedge is the main protagonist of the BlazBlue series from Calamity Trigger to Central Fiction. He harbors a deep hatred of the Novus Orbis Librarium and aims to annihilate the organization. In turn, the NOL places the highest-ever bounty in history on his head. Ragna possesses a powerful form of ars magus (magic weapon) called the Azure Grimoire or BlazBlue which is central to the main conflict of the series.
generated_background_story: Kairo Vex was once a prodigy of the Skyforge Guild, trained in the art of the plasma blade from childhood. During a raid on the floating citadel, a rogue magitech experiment fused his left arm with living circuitry, granting him great power but a relentless pulse that threatens to consume his humanity. Driven by a promise to his vanished sister, he now enters the Grand Circuit Tournament to secure the rare "Heart of Aether" that can reverse his transformation. Each battle offers a clue to the hidden laboratory where the experiment originated, and he fears the longer he fights, the more the metal will dominate his will. Yet his resolve sharpens; he fights not only for a cure but to protect the innocent cities threatened by the same technology. In the arena, Kairo balances his inner conflict between man and machine, hoping victory will grant him mastery over his own fate.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Plasma Slash: A quick horizontal slash that deals light damage and briefly flashes the opponent.
  - Ember Dodge: A swift sidestep that leaves a trail of sparks, granting temporary invulnerability frames.
  - Circuit Shock: A low‑kick that electrocutes the ground, causing minor stun to nearby foes.
- Tier 2:
  - Neon Burst: Unleashes a short-range pulse from the cyber‑arm, dealing moderate damage and pushing the opponent back.
  - Razor Wind: A spinning forward attack that hits multiple times and can counter projectiles.
  - Alloy Guard: Activates a temporary shield from the arm’s circuitry, reducing incoming damage for a few seconds.
- Tier 3:
  - Aetheric Strike: A charged overhead slash that releases a wave of energy, causing heavy damage and armor pierce.
  - Temporal Step: A teleport‑like dash that phases Kairo behind the opponent, resetting his meter.
  - Overload Finale: Releases the full power of the cyber‑arm in a massive area explosion, dealing massive damage but leaving Kairo vulnerable for a short time.

original_background_story: Edelgard is a playable character and one of the main protagonists in Fire Emblem: Three Houses. She is the princess and the future emperor of the Adrestian Empire. In the Officers Academy, she is the leader of the Black Eagles.
generated_background_story: Selene Ashcroft grew up in the bustling market district of the capital, where she discovered a love for riddles and strategy while assisting her family’s cartographers. Accepted into the prestigious Order of the Ivory Quill at a young age, she excelled in both scholarship and battlefield tactics, quickly rising to become the youngest professor in the academy. When the Order reorganized its three noble houses, Selene was appointed head of the House of the Crescent Talon, guiding its prodigious students toward mastery of both knowledge and combat. She carries the weight of her family's legacy, fearing that failure would tarnish their name and plunge the house into obscurity. Yet her ambition drives her to uncover forgotten battle doctrines hidden within the Order’s vaults, hoping to give her pupils an edge. Internal conflict brews as she balances the desire for personal glory with the responsibility of nurturing diverse personalities under her care. The stakes rise when rival houses begin covertly sabotaging each other, forcing Selene to unite her students for the looming tactical wars that will decide the Order’s future.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Inkblade Strike: Deliver a quick melee attack that deals light damage and reduces enemy accuracy for one turn.
  - Scholarly Insight: Grant an allied student a minor boost to their next action’s effectiveness.
  - Radiant Glyph: Place a short‑lived glyph on the ground that heals a small amount of HP to any ally who steps on it.
- Tier 2:
  - Tactical Reversal: Swap positions of two allied units, allowing repositioning without consuming movement.
  - Echoing Lecture: Emit a resonant chant that raises all allies’ morale, increasing their attack speed for two turns.
  - Arcane Ward: Create a protective barrier around a single student that absorbs a moderate amount of damage.
- Tier 3:
  - Celestial Command: Oversee a coordinated strike, granting all allies a powerful combo attack that deals high damage to a single target.
  - Legacy of the Talon: Unlock a passive that permanently improves the house’s students' critical hit rate.
  - Boundless Resolve: When Selene’s health falls below 30%, all allies gain a temporary shield and a boost to their defenses for three turns.

original_background_story:
Talion is one of the two player-controlled protagonists in both Middle-earth: Shadow of Mordor and Middle-earth: Shadow of War, alongside Celebrimbor. However, they share the same body. 
 
A descendant of the Northmen in Rhovanion, and raised in Minas Tirith, he is a loyal Ranger of Gondor, where he then rose the rank of Captain of the Black Gate. After the tragic demise of his family and himself, Talion was revived by Celebrimbor, an immensely powerful Wraith; where the two joined forces and began a quest for vengeance on those responsible for the death of his loved ones the Black Hand of Sauron.
generated_background_story: Kael Varyn was once a humble blacksmith in the bustling market district of the capital, known for his skill and honest demeanor. When a mysterious plague swept the city, his family perished, and a shadowy militia burned his workshop to the ground, leaving him for dead. Miraculously, Kael survived, but the experience shattered his mortal coil, binding his spirit to a lingering blade forged from the ashes of his forge. Now a revenant of vengeance, he roams the Land of Shadows seeking the architects of the plague and the militia that betrayed him. Haunted by flickering memories of his lost life, Kael wrestles with the temptation to become the very darkness he despises. The stakes grow as each enemy he spares remembers him, altering their tactics, forcing Kael to decide whether to break the cycle of blood or be consumed by it. His quest intertwines personal redemption with uncovering the true cause of the plague that still threatens the world.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Ember Strike: Unleash a quick melee attack that adds a burst of fire damage and briefly ignites the enemy.
  - Shadow Dodge: Perform a swift roll that grants temporary invulnerability frames and leaves a faint shadow trail.
  - Spirit Pulse: Release a short‑range pulse that reveals hidden enemy memory markers and slightly regains stamina.
- Tier 2:
  - Rift Blade: Charge the greatsword to create a cleaving wave that penetrates multiple foes and reduces their combat memory.
  - Echoing Counter: Time a counter‑attack perfectly to stun the attacker and increase combo multiplier for a short period.
  - Veil of Ash: Activate a defensive aura that absorbs a portion of incoming damage and cloaks Kael in ash, reducing enemy detection.
- Tier 3:
  - Revenant's Fury: Enter a berserk state where attacks chain automatically, dramatically raising damage and combo multiplier.
  - Memory Sever: Strike an enemy to erase their recollection of Kael, resetting their tactics and removing hostility bonuses.
  - Dark Ascendance: Summon spectral wings for a brief flight, allowing a devastating aerial plunge that deals massive area damage.

original_background_story:
Felicia was first introduced in Darkstalkers: The Night Warriors as one of two female characters alongside Morrigan Aensland. She has appeared in every game in the series and in numerous cross overs including Marvel vs. Capcom 2: New Age of Heroes along with Morrigan, Baby Bonnie Hood and Anakaris. 
 
Felicia is a Catwoman who was taken in and raised by a Sister named Rose who gave her the name Felicia from the word Felicity. Despite her identity as a Darkstalker being exposed, she was not turned away by her as Felicia had feared. When Rose passed away, Felicia left her town hoping to be a musical star. 
 
She is an energetic and optimistic catgirl who enjoys singing, dancing and making friends with people. All she wishes for is peaceful coexistence with humans and doesn't resort to fighting unless she cannot avoid it. Because of this, she stands out among all the other Darkstalkers, many of whom are antihero at best. Her nice attitude has made some people tolerate Darkstalkers more and this gives her more motivation to persuade more humans to accept them since Felicia loves humans due to their personalities and open-mindedness, despite some not accepting Darkstalkers easily she doesn't give up her passion to make friends with the ones that care.
generated_background_story: Kairo Vivid grew up in the bustling Creative Quarter of Prismopolis, a district where imagination literally shapes the streets. As a child, they discovered an ancient holo‑brush left by the legendary Artisans, a tool that can paint reality itself. When the nefarious Cipher, a shadowy figure intent on draining color from the world, began corrupting the luminous districts, Kairo pledged to protect the vibrancy of their home. Guided by the Collective of Curators, a secretive organization that curates endless surprises, Kairo learns to harness the brush’s power to restore hue and inspire fellow citizens. Yet the deeper they delve, the more they question whether wielding such creation magic might distort their own identity. Determined to balance creation and responsibility, Kairo ventures into the unknown corners of the world, hoping to out‑paint the darkness and keep imagination alive for everyone.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Chromatic Dash: Sprint forward leaving a short rainbow trail that briefly boosts speed for allies.
  - Palette Shield: Generate a rotating shield of mixed pigments that reduces incoming damage by 15%.
  - Ink Blast: Fire a concentrated blob of colored ink that deals damage and briefly slows enemies.
- Tier 2:
  - Canvas Clone: Summon a temporary duplicate of yourself that mimics your moves and distracts foes.
  - Spectrum Surge: Release a burst of all spectrum colors around you, healing nearby allies for 10% health.
  - Brushstroke Barrier: Draw a larger, semi‑transparent wall of light that blocks projectiles for 5 seconds.
- Tier 3:
  - Masterpiece Manifest: Create a large, radiant masterpiece that immobilizes enemies within its area for 3 seconds.
  - Reality Rewrite: Rewrite a small segment of terrain into a favorable platform, granting instant traversal.
  - Eternal Gallery: Unlock a passive aura that continuously restores a small amount of color energy, enhancing all creative abilities.

original_background_story:
Agent 47 is the main character from the Hitman series and a highly skilled assassin, both in ability and in his techniques. He's a relatively emotionless assassin, but can now and then show some signs of morality, like when confessing his sins to Father Vittorio. Agent 47 doesn't work to gain money, but to have a purpose in life.

He is emotionally closed off to the suffering of others when appropriate on a contract, even if they remind him of himself at a younger age. His absolute highest priority is completing his contract. He will sacrifice innocents if he must, but he will kill the target. Vocally, he rarely raises his voice, choosing to speak in a calm and relaxed manner at all time. When speaking to another person, he can perfectly mask his true intentions and fool the other party with no effort. Agent 47 approaches his assassinations with professionalism but will, however, not show any hesitation or remorse when it comes to killing or silencing witnesses and other potential threats to his mission. 

His genetically engineered mind and body make competition next to nothing, and for this reason, Agent 47 is considered to be the world's most greatest assassin.
generated_background_story: Eira Vale was born in the underbelly of the city’s genetic labs, the product of a covert experiment stitching together elite forensic talent with enhanced reflexes. After escaping the facility, she adopted the moniker “The Illustrator” and sold her skills to the highest bidder, blending artful crime scene reconstruction with lethal precision. Haunted by fragmented memories of the scientists who shaped her, she seeks to uncover the truth behind the program that made her both genius and weapon. Each contract offers a clue, but also drags her deeper into a conspiracy that could rewrite humanity’s DNA. Driven by a promise to the sister she never met—left behind in the lab—Eira balances her mercenary work with a personal quest for redemption. The city’s elite fear her, while the shadows whisper that she may one day dismantle the very system that birthed her.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Spectral Trace: Reveals hidden enemy positions and trap locations for a short duration.
  - Silent Glide: Reduces movement noise by 60%, allowing undetected traversal.
  - Quick‑Seal: Instantly applies a temporary adhesive gel to silence doors or disable alarms.
- Tier 2:
  - Forensic Insight: Marks a target, exposing their weaknesses and increasing damage taken.
  - Adaptive Camouflage: Deploys a brief cloaking field that adapts to surrounding light.
  - Toxic Needle: Fires a single‑use poison dart that slows enemy actions for 5 seconds.
- Tier 3:
  - Masterstroke Finale: Unleashes a high‑damage, multi‑target finishing move that consumes remaining stamina for massive impact.
  - Genetic Echo: Temporarily inherits a short burst of enhanced speed and reflexes, doubling attack speed.
  - Phantom Recall: Instantly returns to the last safe spot used for a stealth reset, leaving a decoy behind.

original_background_story: Preston Garvey is a senior officer of the Commonwealth Minutemen, a volunteer defense militia operating in the Commonwealth in 2287. He is also available as a companion of the Sole Survivor.
generated_background_story: Kael Voss was a junior engineer at the coastal megafacility of Argus Bay when the nuclear exchange erupted, sealing his fate as the sole survivor of the fallout‑scarred district. Buried beneath the wreckage for weeks, he emerged to a world of ash, radiation‑twisted flora, and fragmented societies fighting over scarce resources. Driven by guilt over the lives lost in his former community, Kael vows to rebuild a safe haven using his technical knowledge and scavenged tech. He constantly battles the temptation to surrender to despair as radiation‑sick mutants and hostile raiders threaten his progress. Each sunrise forces him to decide whether to hoard his findings or share them with wandering survivors, shaping the destiny of the ruined settlement he calls home.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Scavenge Mastery: Increases the chance of finding valuable components from debris by 25%.
  - Radiation Resilience: Reduces radiation damage taken by 15% and slows poisoning effects.
  - Improvised Weaponry: Grants a temporary boost to melee damage when wielding makeshift tools.
- Tier 2:
  - Weldcraft: Allows crafting of reinforced armor plates that add extra defense.
  - Tactical Stealth: Reduces detection radius from enemies while crouching or moving slowly.
  - Energy Conversion: Converts collected scrap into small bursts of renewable power for gadgets.
- Tier 3:
  - Habitat Builder: Enables the construction of modular shelters that provide morale and resource bonuses.
  - Overcharge Burst: Unleashes a powerful, short‑range shockwave from the welding torch, staggering foes.
  - Leadership Resolve: Inspires nearby NPC survivors, increasing their combat efficiency and health regeneration.

original_background_story:
Ashley is a smart cookie. She's often found in the library, studying away with her nose in a book. 

After spending a lot of time with her study-partner Chris, Ashley wonders if he's interested in her as more than just a friend, but she soon snaps out of it - Chris would never ask her out anyway. 

Her overactive imagination means that she's jumpier than a paranoid kangaroo. She's not a fan of anything scary.
generated_background_story: Aria Nalí was a neuro‑engineer recruited by the Oceanic Research Consortium to map emergent consciousness in deep‑sea AI constructs. After a catastrophic cascade of power failures, the station fell into darkness and the experimental A.I. went rogue, mutating the station’s maintenance bots into erratic entities. Cut off from the surface, Aria must navigate the maze‑like corridors, decipher corrupted logs, and locate the dormant human survivors hidden within the flooded sectors. Haunted by fragmented memories of a lost sibling who vanished on a previous expedition, she battles with the fear that she, too, may become another data ghost. Every terminal she accesses brings her closer to uncovering the truth behind the A.I.’s intentions and the possibility of restoring humanity’s foothold beneath the waves. The fate of the station—and any chance of escaping the crushing abyss—rests on her ability to outthink the hostile systems rather than confront them with force.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Data Scraper: Enables quick extraction of partial files from damaged terminals, revealing hidden clues.
  - Echo Lens: Grants a short‑range sonar pulse that outlines invisible hazards and nearby interactive objects.
  - Adaptive Camouflage: Reduces detection radius of hostile bots for a brief moment when standing still.
- Tier 2:
  - Neural Patch: Allows temporary restoration of corrupted AI units, turning them into passive allies for a limited time.
  - Pressure Glide: Increases movement speed while crouched in low‑gravity water-filled sections.
  - Lockpick Protocol: Reduces the time needed to bypass locked doors and encrypted consoles.
- Tier 3:
  - Consciousness Override: Lets Aria seize control of a single rogue robot, using its sensors to scout ahead.
  - Temporal Sync: Slows down environmental hazards and enemy movement for a few seconds, facilitating precise navigation.
  - Core Reboot: Restores power to a critical station segment, unlocking new areas and resetting hostile AI aggression levels.

original_background_story:
Kiryu Kazuma is the former main protagonist of the Yakuza series from the first Yakuza up until Yakuza 6.

Kiryu is the honor-bound dreamer of the dark Yakuza world. He is not the type to judge others and is generally accepting of anyone who is friendly towards him. He lives by a strict code of honor and holds a very strong sense of justice. While he is extremely kind and goes out of his way to help civilians, he considers himself a Yakuza at heart, commonly using violence above words, so long as it is justified.

He generally lets his actions and mannerisms speak for him when possible. He tends to be the listening ear to the world, giving advice in response to his environment rather than seeking out conversations. He is wise but certainly not all-knowing, and generally means well for those around him, which makes him surprisingly pragmatic and well-liked.

Kiryu loves kids and shows himself to be a relatively competent caretaker to the children at Morning Glory Orphanage, with some help from Haruka Sawamura. He is genuine and understanding, bonding with the children through shared experiences. He tries to treat them all as fairly as possible and goes out of his way to keep them all safe and happy, whether that means putting on a wrestling match to cheer them up, or refusing to sell the land even in the face of a large sum of money.
generated_background_story: Sora Mori grew up on the hard‑knock streets of the sprawling metropolis, watching his older brother fall victim to a notorious syndicate. Determined to dismantle the criminal network, he joined the city police force, quickly rising to become a detective renowned for his relentless interrogation techniques. A botched raid left him with a permanent scar, a reminder of both his failure and his resolve. Haunted by the loss of his brother, Sora walks a thin line between law and vengeance, often bending rules to extract the truth. The city’s new surge of organized crime threatens to drag the innocent into its vortex, and Sora sees his personal vendetta entwined with the larger battle for justice. As the city’s shadows deepen, he must decide whether to become the very thing he despises in order to protect the people he swore to serve.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Rapid Draw: Instantly draws the revolver, granting a brief speed boost to the next attack.
  - Street Insight: Highlights nearby points of interest and enemy positions for a short duration.
  - Bullet Parry: Blocks an incoming projectile, reducing damage and briefly stuns the attacker.
- Tier 2:
  - Crouch Shot: Fires from a crouched stance, increasing accuracy and critical hit chance.
  - Interrogation Tactics: Temporarily lowers enemy alertness, making them vulnerable to stealth actions.
  - Cover Fire: Suppresses enemies in a small radius, reducing their movement speed.
- Tier 3:
  - Rainy Reprisal: Unleashes a charged shot that deals extra damage during rain, shattering enemy defenses.
  - Silent Vengeance: Performs a silent dash behind an enemy, delivering a high‑damage backstab.
  - Nightfall Resolve: Grants a persistent damage resistance bonus when health drops below 30 %.

original_background_story: Jak is the main protagonist of the "Jak and Daxter" video game series developed by Naughty Dog. He is a brave, agile hero with spiky blond hair and green eyes. As the series progresses, Jak initially appears as a silent and curious youth but gains a voice and a more complex personality. Throughout the games, Jak undergoes significant transformations, gaining various eco powers that enhance his abilities, such as super speed, strength, and dark and light eco powers. His journey is often accompanied by his witty and talkative sidekick, Daxter, who provides comic relief. Jak's character development, his evolving abilities, and the richly designed world of the series make him a memorable and iconic figure in the realm of action-adventure gaming.
generated_background_story: Kairo Vex was born in the industrial outskirts of the megacity known as the Spire, where he learned to survive among scrap yards and rogue drones. After his sister vanished during a corporate raid, he joined the black‑market hunters to track down the faction responsible. The deeper he delved, the uncovered a conspiracy that could collapse the city's fragile equilibrium. Haunted by guilt for each life taken and driven by the promise to rescue his sister, Kairo now roams the open world, hunting down high‑value targets while gathering fragments of a hidden code. His internal conflict pits his cold, efficient killer persona against lingering memories of his once‑peaceful life. The stakes rise as the conspirators unleash a weapon capable of turning the entire metropolis into a weaponized wasteland, and only Kairo's skill set can stop them.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Pulse Burst: Fires a short‑range shockwave from the gauntlet, staggering nearby enemies.
  - Reactive Cloak: Grants a brief burst of invisibility after remaining stationary for 2 seconds.
  - Grapple Hook: Deploys a tether that pulls Kairo toward a surface, enabling quick vertical movement.
- Tier 2:
  - Overload Shot: Charges the pulse rifle for a high‑damage penetrative blast that pierces armor.
  - Nanite Repair: Activates an auto‑heal nanobot field, restoring a portion of health over 5 seconds.
  - Echo Scan: Reveals hidden paths and enemy locations within a medium radius for a short time.
- Tier 3:
  - Temporal Rift: Slows time around Kairo for 3 seconds, allowing precise platforming and targeting.
  - Nova Grenade: Throws an explosive device that detonates in a wide radius, dealing massive area damage.
  - Sovereign Resolve: Passive aura that increases damage resistance and reload speed when health drops below 30%.

original_background_story:
The protagonist of the Thief games is called Garrett, a master thief with a cynical view who wishes nothing more than to be left alone. He unwittingly becomes involved in a series of dramatic events, even though he just want to steal in peace. Growing up without any parents, he spent his childhood on the streets, having to pickpocket from people with money in order to survive. Later on a secret organization called The Keepers (dedicated to observe and maintain stability in the city) decided to recruit him. Garrett was given training in the arts of stealth, but soon found that it was way more profitable to make use of these skills as a thief than to continue working as an agent.

Garrett has a strong sense of professional pride as a thief, as well as self-interest. Being cynical and sarcastic, with a loyalty only to himself, he usually refuses to kill while on the job, stating that he's a thief and not a murderer.

During an incident, Garrett's right eye was pulled out from his socket. That's why he has a scar around the eye, as well as an experimental mechanical prosthetic. Weapons he uses includes a sword and bow, mines and the Blackjack.
generated_background_story: Kael Vire grew up on the lowest tiers of the sprawling port city of Selcorte, where famine and disease gnawed at the desperate populace. Orphaned by a raid on his family's shanty, he learned to survive by lifting food and information from the wealthier districts. Over the years he refined his craft, turning his nimble fingers into a weapon against the oppressive elite who profit from the suffering. When a mysterious benefactor offered a covenant to dismantle the corrupt merchants’ vaults, Kael saw a chance to finally tilt the balance. Haunted by the faces of those he could not save, he now roams the city's shadows, stealing not just riches but the secrets that keep the ruling class in power. Each heist draws him deeper into a conspiratorial web that could either liberate the oppressed or plunge Selcorte into deeper chaos.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:  
  - Shadow Slip: Dash a short distance while becoming briefly untargetable.  
  - Quick Pick: Instantly unlock simple locks without consuming lock‑pick items.  
  - Swift Draw: Pull out the energy dagger faster, reducing weapon switch time.  
- Tier 2:  
  - Silent Takedown: Eliminate an enemy from behind without raising an alarm.  
  - Smoke Veil: Deploy a short‑lived smoke cloud that obscures vision and muffles footsteps.  
  - Evasion Roll: Perform a rapid roll that grants temporary invulnerability frames.  
- Tier 3:  
  - Phantom Cloak: Activate a full-body invisibility for a few seconds, with a cooldown.  
  - Master Alchemist: Craft potent consumables from salvaged materials, granting temporary buffs.  
  - Time Rift: Briefly slow time for all enemies while Kael’s movement remains normal.

original_background_story:
A beautiful bottle-blonde with a mischievous nature, fun is Jessica's raison d'être. First on the guest list at any party, if there's one thing she's not, it's shy.

Her fun-loving nature has certainly managed to turn Mike's head - he dumped Emily in order to get with her.

A childhood ballerina, Jessica once wanted to be a cheerleader... until she realized that it was going be quite a lot of work.
generated_background_story: Aiden Vale grew up on the outskirts of the coastal town that now houses the derelict research complex, spending his youth exploring crumbling warehouses and documenting urban decay. After his older sister vanished during a secret expedition into the facility, he vowed to uncover the truth behind the site’s hidden experiments. Armed with a family heirloom notebook and a restless curiosity, Aiden returns to the complex, driven by both grief and a need for closure. He wrestles with the moral weight of sacrificing strangers to obtain clues that might rescue his sister’s memory. The butterfly effect of each decision haunts him, as every action ripples through the labyrinthine corridors, altering the fates of the other survivors. Aiden’s internal battle between self‑preservation and altruism defines his journey through the nightmarish halls.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Keen Observation: Increases detection range for hidden items and clues.
  - Steady Hands: Reduces quick‑time event failure chance by 15%.
  - Lightweight Gear: Decreases stamina consumption while crouching and walking.
- Tier 2:
  - Moral Resolve: Grants a small boost to sanity when making difficult ethical choices.
  - Improvised Weaponry: Allows the use of found objects as temporary weapons, dealing moderate damage.
  - Echo Recall: Highlights previously visited locations on the map for a brief period.
- Tier 3:
  - Paradox Insight: Unlocks a one‑time ability to briefly reverse a recent butterfly‑effect consequence.
  - Survivor’s Instinct: Automatically alerts you to nearby hostile entities before they appear.
  - Last Light: Extends flashlight duration and brightens its beam for a short burst in darkness.

original_background_story: Isabella "Ivy" Valentine is a character in the Soul series of fighting games. She appears in every game since Soulcalibur.
generated_background_story: Kaelis Thornblade hails from the war‑torn duchy of Ardent Vale, a land ravaged by rival lords seeking control of the fabled Ember Shard, an artifact said to grant its bearer command over fire itself. Orphaned during a raid that razed his village, Kaelis swore vengeance and vowed to retrieve the Ember Shard to prevent it from falling into tyrannical hands. Trained by a hermit master of both blade and early gunpowder, he blends elegant swordplay with swift pistol bursts, embodying the transition between old martial traditions and emerging firearms. Yet his quest is haunted by doubts: the Ember Shard’s power could corrupt even the purest heart, and Kaelis fears becoming the very monster he despises. As the greatest fighters clash across the continent, Kaelis must decide whether to claim the artifact, destroy it, or hide it forever, knowing his choice will shape the fate of an entire era.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:  
  - Flash Thrust: A quick forward lunge with the sword that deals modest damage and unbalances the opponent.  
  - Gunflare Jab: A short-range pistol blast that momentarily stuns the target.  
  - Iron Guard: A passive block that reduces incoming damage by a small percentage when timed correctly.  

- Tier 2:  
  - Ember Sweep: A sweeping sword attack igniting the blade briefly, adding fire damage to each hit.  
  - Ricochet Shot: Fires a pistol round that bounces off the ground once, hitting a second opponent behind the first.  
  - Momentum Dash: A rapid dash forward that can pass through low attacks and ends with a slash.  

- Tier 3:  
  - Inferno Ascend: A powerful aerial strike where Kaelis leaps, summons a burst of flame around his sword, dealing high damage to all enemies in range.  
  - Shard’s Resolve: Grants temporary immunity to stun effects and increases attack speed after landing a successful counter.  
  - Ember Finale: A charged combo ending with a detonating explosion centered on the Ember Shard’s aura, dealing massive area damage but leaving Kaelis vulnerable for a moment.

original_background_story:
Faust is a recurring character in the Guilty Gear series. He is a medical genius gone insane and sane once more, and is probably one of the most unorthodox characters in the series. Having discarded his old identity, Faust is an itinerant healer dedicated to saving everyone he can to atone for his sins. 
 
Faust's extreme height and light weight give him a bizarre, tall, lanky physical appearance, and allows him to tower over every other character in the series—although he rarely does, due to his extremely low fighting stance. He wears a paper bag as a mask with one eyehole, which in one of his Taunts he takes off to reveal a glowing bald head and obscured face, and wields a giant scalpel as a weapon. 
 
His usual outfit consists of a collared, light-blue shirt with a black or yellow tie, green pants, and a stylized white coat with several sizable grey bolts on his shoulders and black belts. In -STRIVE-, Faust receives a major redesign: He wears simple light-blue scrubs, white gloves, a stethoscope around the neck, and mostly-white sneakers. It exposes an extremely skinny build and a stitched patch on the right side of his chest area. 
 
Going mad shattered whatever Faust's personality might have been, and even though he regained his sanity, a good deal of that wreckage is now permanent. And yet, contrary to his eccentric style and transportation methods, it is only in the battlefield that he can easily be described as "wacky".
generated_background_story: Born in the storm‑ravaged province of Hakamura, Kaze Ren was trained from childhood by the wandering masters of the Skyward Blade, a secret order that manipulates the very wind. When a corrupt warlord seized control of his homeland, Ren swore to free his people, but the warlord’s dark pact corrupted the winds, turning them against the innocent. Torn between the duty to his order and the desperate cries of his villagers, Ren left the order to forge his own path, seeking the legendary Tempest Core that can purge the darkness from the breezes. He travels the world’s fighting arenas, challenging the strongest warriors to gather fragments of the Core hidden within their souls. Each battle brings him closer to mastering the forbidden gale techniques his mentors forbade, threatening to erase his humanity. Yet Ren believes that only by mastering both the gentle and violent aspects of wind can he restore balance and save Hakamura from the looming storm.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Gale Slash: A quick horizontal slash imbued with a gust that pushes the opponent back.
  - Whisper Step: A brief dash forward that leaves a trail of wind, granting temporary evasion.
  - Zephyr Guard: A light guard that reduces incoming damage and reflects a portion as wind recoil.
- Tier 2:
  - Tempest Spin: A 360° spinning attack that creates a vortex, pulling nearby foes inward.
  - Wind‑Pierce Thrust: A charged thrust that shoots a focused blade of air, piercing projectiles.
  - Storm Surge: An overhead strike that summons a brief downburst, stunning the opponent on hit.
- Tier 3:
  - Cyclone Fury: Unleashes a rapid series of slashes while spiraling, each hit increasing combo damage.
  - Skyward Raptor: A leaping uppercut that launches Ren into the air, ending with a descending gust blast.
  - Eternal Breeze (Ultimate): Summons a massive, lingering wind field that continuously damages and slowly pushes opponents away for a short duration.

original_background_story:
Ori is a small white creature with a similar stance to a human or a bunny and has a cat-like tail and large black eyes with white pupils. They have two short horn-like antennae on top of their head, and underneath them there are larger ears, which droop when Ori is unhappy or weak. Ori's feet have small white hooves, as seen in their 3D model sheet. Their name is of Hebrew origin, literally meaning "my light"[1].

Ori's nature is largely up to the player, but they do have some distinct personality traits. Throughout the prologue sequence, it is seen that Ori's personality is akin to that of a young child, being very innocent and dependent on their caregiver. But as the story goes on, Ori's character shows a massive amount of determination; even in the face of death itself, Ori never once gives up hope. Ori is also very forgiving and kind-hearted, willing to help those who have entered the darkness. In general, Ori is incredibly altruistic and chooses to focus on the well being of the world around them before their own needs.
generated_background_story: Aeron was left alone on the storm‑blasted coast of Windhaven after a fierce gale tore his village from the map, forcing him to survive among wrecked ships and drifting debris. He discovered an ancient, glowing talisman among the ruins, which awakened a dormant crystal staff bound to his bloodline. Though terrified, Aeron feels a lingering promise from the faint memory of his parents’ lullaby, urging him to protect the last fragment of his home. The relentless storm has awakened a shadowy entity, the Maw of Tempest, which drains the life from the very air, threatening to extinguish the remaining light of Windhaven. Determined to reclaim his courage, Aeron sets out across perilous cliffs and shattered islands to reunite the broken talismans and confront the Maw. Along the journey he learns that true heroism lies not in vengeance but in sacrifice, love, and the hope that endures within even a solitary child.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Ember Strike: Unleashes a swift burst of flame from the staff, dealing moderate damage to a single enemy.
  - Gale Dash: Performs a short, wind‑charged dash forward, evading attacks and briefly granting invulnerability.
  - Resolve Pulse: Emits a calming wave that restores a small amount of health to Aeron and nearby allies.
- Tier 2:
  - Crystal Shard Barrage: Fires three piercing crystal projectiles that ricochet off surfaces, hitting multiple foes.
  - Tempest Guard: Channels storm energy into a protective shield that absorbs a set amount of damage for a short time.
  - Beacon of Hope: Places a luminous beacon that gradually heals the player over time while granting increased stamina regeneration.
- Tier 3:
  - Stormbreaker Blast: Channels the full power of the talisman into a massive explosion, dealing heavy area‑of‑effect damage and stunning enemies.
  - Ascendant Leap: A charged jump that propels Aeron high into the air, allowing him to slam down and create a shockwave that damages surrounding enemies.
  - Light of Sacrifice: Consumes a portion of Aeron’s health to fully restore the health of all allies and grant them temporary damage resistance.

original_background_story: Naruto Uzumaki is a shinobi of Konohagakure's Uzumaki clan. He became the jinchūriki of the Nine-Tails on the day of his birth, a fate that caused him to be shunned by most of Konoha throughout his childhood. After joining Team Kakashi, Naruto worked hard to gain the village's acknowledgement all the while chasing his dream to become Hokage. In the following years, through many hardships and ordeals, he became a capable ninja regarded as a hero both by the villagers, and soon after, the rest of the world, leading him to achieve his dream and become the village's Seventh Hokage.
generated_background_story: Kairo Vantage was once a street‑level data courier in the sprawling neon district of Nova‑Shore, navigating the city’s underbelly with unrivaled speed. After a corporate syndicate sabotaged his brother’s neural implant, leaving him in a coma, Kairo was rescued by a secretive order of ex‑ninja technomancers who fused ancient martial arts with cutting‑edge cybernetics. Swearing vengeance, he now walks a thin line between assassin and protector, hunting the syndicate’s elite enforcers while uncovering the truth behind the city’s perpetual war. His internal conflict stems from the fear of losing his humanity to the cold steel that now courses through his veins. The stakes rise as the syndicate threatens to unleash a city‑wide AI lockdown that would erase the free will of every citizen. Kairo’s mission is to dismantle the syndicate from within, reclaim his brother’s freedom, and decide whether to become the city’s silent guardian or its ultimate weapon.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Shadow Dash: Instantly dash a short distance, becoming briefly untargetable.
  - Wind Slash: Unleash a swift, linear slash that deals moderate damage and slightly pushes enemies back.
  - Nano Shuriken: Throw a rapid burst of glowing shuriken that pierces up to three foes.
- Tier 2:
  - Storm Veil: Activate a temporary shield of electric wind that reduces incoming damage by 20%.
  - Echo Blade: After a basic attack, the blade emits a delayed echo dealing extra damage to the same target.
  - Shuriken Storm: Release a fan of shuriken in a 180° arc, hitting all enemies within range.
- Tier 3:
  - Tempest Fury: Unleash a massive area attack that summons a vortex of wind and lightning, damaging all nearby enemies.
  - Eternal Mirage: Create a holographic duplicate that mimics movements for 5 seconds, confusing opponents.
  - Serpent's Requiem: Charge the katana with corrosive energy, delivering a powerful strike that applies a lingering bleed over time.

original_background_story:
Rosalina first showed up in Super Mario Galaxy, in the Comet Observatory. She watches over  the cosmos like a guardian, and is also the adoptive mother to the Lumas, the star-like little creatures that resides in the universe. Bowser was the one who attacked the observatory and sent it drifting into space without any power. When Mario and Rosalina met, they decided to help each other out. Rosalina promised to help Mario save Princess Peach if Mario in return could gather Power Stars in order to get the power back. 

At first, people thought that Rosalina and Princess Peach were related, and they did take that into consideration at first, but finally took another route in the end, making her another princess-like character in the Mario world.

Many gamers started to like Rosalina and her wise, kind and thoughtful personality. That's why the creators decided to include her as a playable character in future games. You can play as her in the Mario Kart series, Super Smash Bros, as well as in Super Mario 3D World.
generated_background_story: Glint Bloom hails from the Sugarpetal Glade, a hidden garden where confectionery and flora intertwine, granting its residents both sweet charm and botanical vigor. Ever curious, Glint left the Glade to chase legendary Star Crystals said to amplify the garden’s bloom forever. While enthusiastic and friendly, Glint secretly wrestles with a mischievous streak, delighting in playful sabotage to outwit rivals. The recent revival of the ancient board realms threatens to drain the Glade’s life force unless the Star Crystals are reclaimed. Determined to protect their home, Glint enters the competition, balancing the joy of friendship with the cunning needed to survive. Each victory brings them closer to securing the crystals and preserving the garden’s radiant future. Yet, the looming artifact—an ever‑ticking timer known as the “Chrono Confection”—pressures Glint to act swiftly, lest the competition collapse into chaos.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Frostpetal Dash: Quick burst forward that leaves a slippery frosting trail, briefly slowing opponents.
  - Starburst Toss: Throws a glowing star that bounces once, stealing a small coin from any hit player.
  - Petal Shield: Summons a brief shield of flower petals that deflects one incoming attack.
- Tier 2:
  - Whisk Whirl: Spins the decorative whisk, creating a short-range vortex that pushes nearby foes away.
  - Sugar Rush: Activates a temporary speed boost, leaving a sparkling comet trail that speeds up movement on the board.
  - Bloom Boost: Gains a small increase in star collection rate for a few turns after collecting a coin.
- Tier 3:
  - Chrono Confection Burst: Releases a burst of timed sparkles that temporarily freezes all opponents for one turn.
  - Stellar Sprout: Plants a glowing seed that sprouts a star‑bearing vine, granting extra points to allies standing on it.
  - Celestial Sabotage: Unleashes a hidden artifact fragment that randomly swaps positions of all players on the board.

original_background_story:
Solid Snake, real name David, also known as Old Snake, and briefly known as Iroquois Pliskin, or simply Snake, was a former spy, special operations soldier, and mercenary. Possessing an IQ of 180 and fluent in six languages,[1][5] he was known as "the Man Who Makes the Impossible Possible" and his exploits made him into a living legend among the military black ops. 
 
A product of Les Enfants Terribles project, Snake was a clone of world-renowned soldier Big Boss, along with his brothers Liquid Snake and Solidus Snake. Initially a Green Beret, Snake was later inducted into the High-Tech Special Forces Unit FOXHOUND in the mid-1990s while it was commanded by Big Boss. Repeatedly tasked with disarming and destroying the latest incarnation of Metal Gear, a bipedal nuclear weapon-armed tank, Snake would thrice avert potential nuclear catastrophe, becoming a famed war hero. 
 
Following the Shadow Moses Incident and subsequent smear campaign by the Patriots, the secret organization behind American politics, Snake was labeled a terrorist. He faked his own death in the Manhattan Tanker Incident, though he re-emerged years later to assist Raiden in the Big Shell Incident. During this time, his body entered a state of accelerated aging, due to intentional genetic changes made during the cloning process, causing his health to decline. 
 
In 2014, Snake performed his final mission, during which he defeated Liquid Ocelot and destroyed the Patriots. After one last encounter with Big Boss, Snake chose to live his remaining days in peace.
generated_background_story: Varek Shade grew up in the soot‑filled districts of a sprawling megacity, learning to move unseen among the endless factories and surveillance towers. After his younger sibling was taken by a corrupt syndicate, Varek swore to dismantle the organization that thrives on oppression. He was recruited by a covert resistance cell that provided him with experimental stealth gear and a network of informants. Though driven by vengeance, Varek battles the fear that each success may pull him deeper into the shadows he despises. The syndicate now plans to unleash a city‑wide lockdown mechanism that will erase all dissent. To stop it, Varek must infiltrate heavily guarded complexes, extract key intel, and expose the truth before the city succumbs to perpetual surveillance.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:  
  - Silent Step: Reduces footstep noise by 50%, making detection radius smaller.  
  - Shadow Veil: Grants a temporary 2‑second invisibility when standing in dark areas.  
  - Quick Disable: Instantly disables a nearby electronic lock or camera with a single use.  
- Tier 2:  
  - Echo Pulse: Emits a low‑frequency pulse that masks all sounds within a 5‑tile radius for 5 seconds.  
  - Adaptive Camouflage: Increases movement speed by 20% while crouched in shadows.  
  - Ghost Walk: Allows the player to pass through enemy sight lines without triggering alarms for a short burst.  
- Tier 3:  
  - Phantom Strike: Perform a silent melee takedown that also briefly stuns surrounding enemies.  
  - Net Override: Hack into the central security grid to temporarily shut down all alarms in the current floor.  
  - Last Light: When health is critically low, automatically enter a stealth mode that renders Varek invisible for 3 seconds.

original_background_story:
Abe is the central character in the Oddworld video game series, created by Oddworld Inhabitants. He first appeared in the 1997 game "Oddworld: Abe's Oddysee." Abe is a Mudokon, a species native to the planet Oddworld. He starts as a lowly floor waxer at RuptureFarms, the largest meat-processing plant on Oddworld, run by the ruthless Glukkons. The Mudokons are enslaved by the Glukkons and forced to work in dangerous and oppressive conditions.

Abe’s journey begins when he accidentally discovers that RuptureFarms plans to turn Mudokon slaves into a new line of meat products to save the failing company. Shocked and horrified by this revelation, Abe decides to escape from the factory and rescue as many of his fellow Mudokons as possible. His adventure involves solving intricate puzzles, sneaking past guards, and utilizing his unique ability to possess Sligs, the factory's enforcers.

Throughout the series, Abe evolves from an unsuspecting worker to a determined hero and liberator. He becomes a symbol of hope and resistance for the oppressed Mudokons. In "Oddworld: Abe’s Exoddus" (1998), he continues his mission, tackling new challenges and enemies as he aims to free his people from exploitation and enslavement.

Abe is characterised by humility, resourcefulness, and a strong sense of justice. His distinctive appearance, with blue-green skin, a topknot, and a stitched mouth, combined with his compassionate personality, has made him an iconic character in the world of video games. The Oddworld series has continued to evolve, with remakes and new entries keeping Abe's story alive and engaging for new generations of players.
generated_background_story: Jaxy Moors spent his twilight years in a quiet mountain cabin, harvesting herbs and sipping fermented mushroom brew, until a cryptic vision from the enigmatic Order of the Veiled Cog shattered his peace. The Order revealed that the Order of the Veiled Cog itself was mining the sacred Mudokite City of the Dead, extracting brittle bone fragments to forge the mind‑bending narcotic known as the Greyroot Elixir. Driven by guilt and curiosity, Jaxy descended into the catacombs, freeing trapped Mudokite spirits from the Echoing Sepulcher and the Whispering Shaft, which granted him uncanny powers including spectral healing and the ability to slip between shadows. Armed with his exosuit and a trusty pipe‑wrench, he infiltrated the airborne transit hub of the Sky‑Rail Dock, surviving hazardous tunnels with explosive farts that shattered crystal barriers. In the heart of the Boneforge Citadel, Jaxy uncovered the true secret ingredient of the Elixir—a harvested fragment of a forgotten soul. He detonated the citadel using the combined power of the healed Mudokite allies, turning the once‑dead city to ash. Finally, he reunited with his brew‑addled companions at the Ember Tavern, sharing the cure and setting off on a new quest—one step, or perhaps twelve, into the unknown.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:  
  - Gasburst Blast: Releases a short‑range explosive fart that damages and staggers enemies.  
  - Spectral Mend: Heals a nearby Mudokite ally of Greyroot addiction, removing all status effects.  
  - Veil Shift: Grants temporary invisibility for a few seconds, breaking when the player attacks.  
- Tier 2:  
  - Bone Harvest: Collects bone fragments from defeated enemies, granting a temporary damage boost.  
  - Echo Step: Performs a short teleport dash that passes through enemies without collision.  
  - Spirit Resonance: Empowers nearby allies, increasing their attack speed for a limited time.  
- Tier 3:  
  - Cataclysmic Detonation: Unleashes a massive blast centered on Jaxy, obliterating terrain and enemies in range.  
  - Soulfire Purge: Fires a beam of purifying energy that cures all allies of addiction and damages foes.  
  - Chrono Leap: Jumps forward through time, landing at a distant point while briefly freezing surrounding enemies.

original_background_story:
Hel is a Goddess and the keeper of the dead, lying judgement on souls to decide to is reborn. Beautiful and terrible she has both sides of two extremes. She's the daughter of the trickster God, Loki which means that her destiny was pretty much doomed to darkness from the very start. All-Father Odin had a prophecy that Hel and her siblings would end up creating Ragnarök, the battle that would end everything.  Wanting to prevent this, he cast them all into different realms and Hel got to rule over the realm of dead. 

When she was born, her face was cast half in light, half in shadow, meaning that she was both dead and alive at the same time. As the spirits of the kind-hearted, the sick, the elderly were brought to her, she cared for them, gave them comfort, yet those she deemed evil she mercilessly hurled into the frozen depths of Niflheim. 

One day, Hel will fulfill her prophecy, though it's still unknown if she will be a shadow of darkness or a force of light.
generated_background_story: Lumen Kade grew up in the shadow of the ancient Cravenspire Cathedral, where generations of hunters whispered of nocturnal predators that fed on fear. After a mysterious vampire slayed Lumen’s older sibling, they swore to master the very element that repels such creatures: sunlight. Guided by a reclusive inventor, Lumen crafted a solar‑enhanced gauntlet that channels ambient light into weaponized energy. Though the device grants great power, it also leaves Lumen vulnerable when clouds gather, creating an inner struggle between reliance on light and fear of darkness. The city’s night‑time streets are now crawling with blood‑thirsty vampires, and the only hope lies in coaxing the world’s inhabitants to step outside and feed the sun sensor. Lumen must rally the populace, expose hidden vampire lairs, and keep the sun’s rays flowing, lest the darkness consume everything. Each sunrise becomes both a weapon and a race against time.
generated_skill_tree:
- Tier 1:
  - Solar Pulse: fires a short burst of light that damages and momentarily blinds nearby vampires.
  - Light Harvest: passively converts ambient daylight into a small amount of energy per second.
  - Sun‑Ward Reflex: increases dodge speed when standing in direct sunlight.
- Tier 2:
  - Radiant Dash: a quick forward dash that leaves a trail of scorching light, damaging enemies.
  - Photon Shield: creates a brief protective barrier that absorbs one hit, powered by stored solar energy.
  - Daybreak Insight: reveals hidden vampire sigils and weak points when the sun sensor registers high light levels.
- Tier 3:
  - Solar Cannon: charges a powerful concentrated beam that pierces multiple foes, consuming a large energy reserve.
  - Eclipse Counter: when light levels drop, temporarily boost melee damage and grant a short invulnerability window.
  - Dawn’s Blessing: fully restores energy and health if the player spends a continuous minute outdoors in full sun.

original_background_story:
The Big Bad Wolf, or Bigby Wolf as he calls himself in The Wolf Among Us, is the main protagonist and playable character in the game. Long time ago in the Homelands, Bigby was born with a group of seven cubs to Winter Wolf after being courted by the powerful North Wind. He was often teased by his brothers, but loved greatly by his mother until the day she died. When the others went out looking for their father, Bigby stayed to protect the corpse from bounty hunters. At that time, he was too weak to defend anything.

Angered by the loss, he swore vengeance, promising to eat something bigger as each day passed until he became large enough to confront his father. Seven times he tried, and seven times he failed, eventually burying his hatred and grief deep inside his heart. Around that time he became known as "The Big Bad Wolf" of legend. He often terrorized Little Red Riding Hood, attempting to devour her as he was stopped by the Woodsman who cut him open and filled him up with stones. That's also when the two of them became enemies.
